## [00:00 - 03:05]

Hi, this is Don Crowther with an alternative to the reframe in your webinar called the opportunity. Now, here's the challenge. Not everyone can come up with a good reframe. It may be that the solution that you're doing is not so much a reframe of the entire problem and the opportunity. It's a better way to deal with that particular problem. And so that's why we give this section called the opportunity that replaces the reframe. So same timing, 48 minutes for 60 minutes, five to 10 minutes for a 90 minute webinar. The opportunity is basically saying, here's the current situation, here's how that affects the world, but there is a way to fix that. And here's how the world will be different as a result. Now, when would you want to do this instead of doing a reframe? Just so that you know, this is actually easier than doing a reframe. A reframe is actually superior to just doing an opportunity. But if you can't get there, then you would do an opportunity statement. So when would you do choose to do this? Well, first off, when the solution is more obvious, they're just missing the pieces to achieve it. So you don't necessarily need to reframe this particular issue. You just the problem has always been there. Now you've got the solution that they have been looking for for all these years. Another time is when what you're presenting is more evolutionary than revolutionary. So this is the solution that's been done in the past. And if we just do it this way, it actually works better than it ever has. Or when there's no obvious aha moment that you can use to hook the audience, you haven't been able to come up with with a total reframe kind of concept. Now, there is a risk of doing the opportunity, which is that your presentation actually when you do an opportunity sale as opposed to a reframe is an opportunity sale is much more logical. A reframe, one of the powerful elements of a reframe is that it's a very emotional experience when you properly go through a reframe, you are psyched afterwards because you're seeing a new way of looking at the world at the problem of the solution. And so it's very emotional moment. When you do an opportunity, it's likely to be much more logical. And so you have to actually work to bring in those emotional aspects into what you're saying. And the reason we're doing this is that emotions sell. Logic is what you use to justify purchasing based upon the emotions you've already created. And so the more emotions we can build into this process,

the more likely we are to close the cell in the end.
